intitule: Lesson 22: Plural Forms and Negation - Resource Exercise

enonce.texte:
Exercise 1: Resource Task
This exercise helps you review the rules of irregular plural nouns and negative forms/contractions in English. Refer to your lesson notes to complete the tasks below.

questions.texte: Complete the table below by writing the correct irregular plural form of each singular noun.
Rewrite each sentence in the negative form using the contraction given in brackets. Identify the auxiliary verb you need to add.

sousQuestions.texte: I am ready to leave. (I'm not)

questions.texte: Identify and correct the mistake in each sentence. The mistake can be either an incorrect plural form or a wrong negative construction.

sousQuestions.texte: The childs are playing in the garden.
sousQuestions.reponse: The children are playing in the garden.

sousQuestions.texte: She don't want to go to the market.
sousQuestions.reponse: She doesn't want to go to the market.

sousQuestions.texte: My tooths are hurting after eating that candy.
sousQuestions.reponse: My teeth are hurting after eating that candy.

sousQuestions.texte: He didn't went to school yesterday.
sousQuestions.reponse: He didn't go to school yesterday.

intitule: Lesson 22: Plural Forms and Negation - Competency Exercise

enonce.texte:
Exercise 2: Competency Task
Read the text below about a day in Douala. Then complete the tasks that follow. Apply your knowledge of irregular plurals and negative forms to correct mistakes and express ideas accurately.
images: A busy market scene in Douala, Cameroon, with people selling fruits, vegetables, and fish. Some women are carrying baskets on their heads.

questions.texte: Read the paragraph below. There are 8 mistakes in plural forms and negative constructions. Find and correct them.

sousQuestions.texte: Original: 'Every morning, the womans go to the market. They buy fishs and vegetable. The childs don't wants to carry the basket. The mens sell clothings. The market don't open on Sunday. The foots of the buyers are tired.'
sousQuestions.reponse: Corrected: 'Every morning, the women go to the market. They buy fish and vegetables. The children don't want to carry the basket. The men sell clothes. The market doesn't open on Sunday. The feet of the buyers are tired.'

questions.texte: Rewrite the following affirmative sentences into negative sentences. Use the appropriate contracted form.

sousQuestions.texte: The people of Cameroon are bilingual (English and French). (Make negative: some people in remote areas)
sousQuestions.reponse: Some people in remote areas aren't bilingual.

sousQuestions.texte: The children have finished eating eru and plantain. (Make negative)
sousQuestions.reponse: The children haven't finished eating eru and plantain.

sousQuestions.texte: The oxen will pull the cart tomorrow. (Make negative)
sousQuestions.reponse: The oxen won't pull the cart tomorrow.

questions.texte: Create three original sentences about life in your community. Use at least one irregular plural noun and one negative contraction in each sentence.

sousQuestions.reponse: Example: The children don't like to eat bitter leaves.
Example: The women haven't sold all the fish at the market.
Example: The people won't walk to Kribi because their feet are tired.

questions.texte: Analyze the following situation and write an appropriate response using a negative sentence. You are a teacher in Yaoundé. A student says: 'All the students have done their homework.' But you know that two students (a boy and a girl) have not. Respond correctly.
questions.reponse: Example: 'No, two children haven't done their homework.' OR 'The boy and the girl haven't done their homework.'
